_comment: P37 North Carolina jurisdictional overlays (western NC focus: Buncombe/Asheville + Rutherford; optional bucket, advisory only). Attribute thresholds are cited statutory/ordinance facts. Geometry records carry a geometry_ref to the authoritative endpoint plus an ILLUSTRATIVE bbox only. Corrections baked in from verified research: Rutherford has NO steep-slope ordinance / no countywide zoning; Asheville is Code Sec. 7-9-2; the MRPA ridgeline layer is topo-map-digitized (screening only). Never a legal or permitting determination.
title: Mountain Ridge Protection Act - protected ridge test (statutory)
text: Under the NC Mountain Ridge Protection Act, a protected mountain ridge is one whose elevation is at least 3,000 feet AND at least 500 feet above the adjacent valley floor; a 'tall building' over 40 feet on such a ridge is restricted where a county or city has opted into the Act. Attribute test on site elevation and height above valley. Advisory only; the opting jurisdiction and a survey are authoritative.

title: MRPA ridgeline proximity (screening layer)
text: Whether a project point is near a mapped protected ridgeline. The NC OneMap MRPA ridgelines layer is topo-map digitized and is SCREENING ONLY; a real determination uses the statutory elevation test plus LiDAR/survey. Resolve the boundary via the live connector or user-supplied data; the bbox below is an ILLUSTRATIVE western-NC placeholder, not a real ridgeline.

title: Buncombe County Steep Slope / High Elevation Overlay
text: Buncombe County (FIPS 37021) Sec. 78-644 Steep Slope/High Elevation Overlay applies to land above 2,500 feet elevation; structures must sit on soils with slope no greater than 35%. Attribute test on county + elevation; the mapped county overlay layer and DEM-derived slope are authoritative. Advisory only.

title: Buncombe County geotechnical analysis trigger (steep grade)
text: In Buncombe County, development on existing grade of 36% or greater (or on parcels mapped High/Moderate hazard on the county Slope Stability Index Map) requires geotechnical analysis by an NC-registered PE. Attribute test on slope percent; the hazard map is the alternative trigger. Advisory only; DEM-derived slope and the county map are authoritative.

title: Rutherford County - no steep-slope ordinance (documentation)
text: Rutherford County (FIPS 37161) has NO countywide zoning and NO steep-slope/hillside/ridge-protection ordinance. Slope controls there reduce to the Watershed Protection, Flood Damage Prevention, Subdivision, and Solar ordinances; any hillside control in the county seat comes from Town of Rutherfordton zoning, a separate jurisdiction. This record exists so the engine does NOT falsely apply a steep-slope overlay to Rutherford. Advisory only.

title: Asheville local historic district / downtown design review
text: Whether a project point falls inside an Asheville local historic overlay or the downtown design review district (Asheville Code Sec. 7-9-2), triggering design review. The authoritative boundary is the city ArcGIS layer; resolve via the live connector or user-supplied GeoJSON. The bbox below is an ILLUSTRATIVE downtown-Asheville placeholder only. Advisory only.

title: NC water-supply watershed protection overlay
text: Whether a project point falls inside an NC DEQ water-supply-watershed (WS) protection overlay (built-upon-area and buffer limits) or a designated river basin (e.g. French Broad, Broad). The authoritative boundary is the NC OneMap / DEQ layer; resolve via the live connector or user-supplied GeoJSON. Advisory only.
